economic_notes: Nation has extensive forestry and logging operations (~$7.7M revenue), Rio Tinto revenue of $17.4M (likely impact benefit agreement or settlement), BCFN Gaming revenue, multiple GBEs including forest products, enterprises, wilderness resort, aggregates, contractors, and property management. Silviculture liability of $4,305,478. COVID-19 impacts noted.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for three reasons: (1) Cheslatta Community Trust not consolidated and trust-creation funds not recognized as revenue; (2) GBEs not prepared under IFRS as required by PSAS; (3) opening tangible capital asset historical costs not provided so existence/completeness/valuation of TCA cannot be verified. Auditor firm name not clearly stated beyond 'Chartered Professional Accountants, Kamloops, BC' — no firm name given. Comparative figures restated (Note 11). Rio Tinto revenue of $17.4M is unusually large and likely relates to a resource impact benefit agreement or compensation — may be partially one-time in nature but not explicitly described as such. 17 GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. Silviculture liability of $4,305,478 is a significant off-balance-sheet-style obligation. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($1,494,892) + FNHA ($74,691) + Province of BC ($941,560) + PGNAETA ($123,908) + FNESC ($48,592) + CSFS ($553,322) + First Nations Health Authority already included = $3,282,804 — note BCFN Gaming $345,439 also potentially government transfer, adjusted total_government_transfers to $3,582,804 if included. osr = total revenue minus total_government_transfers.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
